content_markdown: A black body emits radiation with its greatest intensity at a wavelength of $\lambda_{\text{max}}$. The surface temperature of the black body doubles without any other change occurring. What is the wavelength at which the greatest intensity of radiation is emitted?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: According to Wien's displacement law, the wavelength of maximum intensity $\lambda_{\text{max}}$ is inversely proportional to the absolute temperature $T$ of the black body ($\lambda_{\text{max}} \propto \frac{1}{T}$). If the temperature doubles ($2T$), the new peak wavelength must be half of the original value.
